rulings.Case Title: ELEANOR YOUNG VS. SHARON YETER ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Defendant Sharon Yeter For Attorneys Fees; **Court Reporter Fees Paid**
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for August 3, 2021 line 1. DEFENDANT SHARON YETER Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Defendant Sharon Yeter For Attorneys Fees is DENIED. Moving party seeks attorney's fees based on a contract to which Mr. Lindley was not a party. No authority is cited for the proposition that Mr. Lindley's share should be reduced by the amount of attorney's fees incurred by Young and Yeter because Young and Yeter had an agreement to that effect. No other basis for recovery of fees is cited. The Court further notes that the relief sought (award of fees) is inconsistent with the Court's January 21, 2021 order and appears to be subject to CCP 1008, which is not addressed in the moving papers. Furthermore, had the Court been aware of the existence of the Young-Yeter agreement and their claims to be reimbursed for fees out of Lindley's share at January 21, 2021 the Court would have been concerned about equity (CCP 874.010) and reasonableness of $80,000 in cumulative fees in this uncontested partition action. Finally, the Court has now become aware that Mr. Lindley died and propriety of proceeding in his absence with an award of fees under a contract that was not the basis for the Complaint upon which the default was entered, is not addressed by the moving defendant. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.
